MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Emilie M. Calhoun, Esq., of the Law Offices of Emilie Calhoun, Tel. 415.759.8765, partition referee and moving party.
Russell M. Davis, Esq., of the Law Offices of Russell Davis, Tel. 415.409.5627, appearing for Petitioner.
R. Andrew Murray, Esq., of the Law Offices of R. Andrew Murray, Tel. 916.375.9831, appearing for Defendant.
Monica Zimmerman, Tel. 415.971.7171, also present in court.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. EMILIE CALHOUN'S Motion For Order Confirming Sale Of Real Property is Granted. No opposition filed. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.

ATTY. R. ANDREW MURRAY, TEL. NO. (916) 375-9831 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-13-2011 Set For Trial On Jul-23-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Ntc Of Motion For Order To Disqualify Pltf'S Counsel And All Attorneys Associated With Him
Off Calendar Dec-05-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RUSSELL M. DAVIS, TEL. NO. 409-5627 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. R. ANDREW MURRAY, TEL. NO. (916) 453-9580 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. DEFENDANT KENDRA STACK'S Motion For Order To Disqualify Plaintiff'S Counsel And All Attorneys Associated With Him IS Denied. No attorney-client relationship was ever established between defendant and Mr. Davis or his office. However, even if there was some fiduciary duty or expectation of confidentiality that arose by virtue of Mr. Davis's procuring the hard money loan, defendant has not shown that she provided any confidential information to Mr. Davis. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Court Reporter: Lavena Ward, CSR #7077 =302/HEK

Plaintiff Kathleen Stack'S Motion For Interlocutory Judgment Of Partition (Part Two)
Off Calendar Nov-03-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff Kathleen Stack's Motion For Interlocutory Judgment Of Partition (Part Two) - See complete ruling under part one. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Interlocutory Judgment
Off Calendar Nov-03-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF KATHLEEN STACK'S MOTION FOR INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT OF PARTITION (PART ONE).

APPEARANCES: Russell M. Davis, Esq. of Law Offices of Russell Davis 409-5627 for pltf & moving party; no other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Granted. The property shall be sold. The proceeds of the sale shall be divided in accordance with each party's 50% interest in the property. The costs of the sale, including the costs of the referee, shall also be evenly divided between the parties. The following three persons are nominated as possible referees: 1) Michael Kasolas (992-5806), who is the husband of a court employee that does not work in department 302; 2) Everett Hewlett (772-0900), who is a former commissioner of the court; and 3) Emilie Calhoun (759-8755), an attorney who handles cases that are in the court's probate department. The parties may contact the nominees to learn about the nominees' rates, qualifications and whether any of them have had any contacts with any of the parties, their counsel or others who have been involved in this case, but may not make inquiries beyond these areas. The parties have until November 14 to choose of these three or anyone else they can agree on to be the referee.
If the parties do not agree on a referee by November 14, then each party may, by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org without copying the other side, on or before November 16 strike one of the nominees and the court will thereafter appoint a referee from the nominee(s) not stricken. The referee is empowered to hire a real estate broker and, in consultation with that broker, to decide the method (sale of the entire property or split up) that is likely to achieve the maximum net proceeds for the parties. Neither Charlene Delaney nor Keller Williams Realty shall be selected as the real estate broker. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
